target.name: Majestic Labs
target.founder: Ofer Shacham, Sha Rabii, Masumi Reynders

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Majestic Labs is a Series A chip design company founded by Ofer Shacham, Sha Rabii, and Masumi Reynders, having raised $100M+ to date. The semiconductors sector encompasses a comparable universe of 3 public benchmarks (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers, with 4 directly comparable Series A-stage companies informing this analysis.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $100M raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, implies a $200M valuation. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same-stage companies—Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), Substrate ($1.0B), and Rain AI ($500M)—yields a $1.5B median, with a range of $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied; while the semiconductors sector median trades at 24.4x price-to-sales across the three public comps, Majestic Labs' revenue is not on file, precluding revenue-multiple methodology.

The triangulation across two methods supports a valuation range of $170M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $850M. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion in private peer valuations, particularly the outsized positions of Etched and d-Matrix relative to smaller peers, and the absence of revenue data, which limits calibration to public-market multiples. The stage-progression method implies material downside to the peer median, warranting scrutiny of Majestic Labs' differentiation within the cohort.
